title: Project Scaffolding & Dependencies
goal: Establish the project directory structure, dependency manifest, environment template, and entry point so that all subsequent parts have a consistent foundation to build on.

in_scope: Create the src/ directory with an empty app.py entry point
Create requirements.txt with all MVP dependencies
Create the static pricing data file placeholder (model_pricing.json)
Set up .gitignore entries for Python, Streamlit, and .env
out_of_scope: Writing any business logic or SDK calls
Streamlit UI code beyond a minimal placeholder

outputs: src/app.py — minimal Streamlit entry point that runs without error
checklist: Create src/app.py with a minimal Streamlit page title ('Azure Foundry LLM Arena')
Create requirements.txt listing: streamlit, azure-ai-projects, azure-ai-inference, azure-identity, python-dotenv
Create .env.example with placeholder entries for AZURE_FOUNDRY_ENDPOINT and AZURE_FOUNDRY_API_KEY
Create src/model_pricing.json as an empty JSON object ({})
acceptance_criteria: Running 'streamlit run src/app.py' launches the app with a page title and no errors
requirements.txt contains all necessary packages with version constraints
.gitignore prevents .env and Python artifacts from being committed
The project structure matches the proposed folder layout
review_focus: Dependency versions are compatible and not overly restrictive
Folder structure supports clean separation of concerns
risks_or_notes: azure-ai-projects and azure-ai-inference SDK packages may have version compatibility requirements — verify latest stable versions
Streamlit version should be >= 1.28 for stable column and session state features
definition_of_done: All listed files exist in the repository
streamlit run src/app.py launches successfully with no import errors
pip install -r requirements.txt completes without conflicts
No secrets or credentials are present in any committed file
